Question: Practical geometry involves drawing shapes using instruments.
A compass is used to draw circles.
A ruler is used to measure lengths.
A protractor is used to measure angles.
A set square is used to draw right angles.
A point has no length
A line has infinite length but no breadth.
A line segment has definite length.
A ray has one endpoint and extends infinitely in one direction.
An angle is formed by two rays with a common endpoint.
A perpendicular bisector divides a line segment into two equal parts at 90°
A perpendicular from a point on a line is drawn at 45°
The bisector of an angle divides the angle into two equal parts.
An equilateral triangle has three equal sides.
A square has four equal sides and four right angles.
A rectangle has opposite sides equal.
A rhombus has four equal sides.
A trapezium has one pair of parallel sides.
A kite has two pairs of equal adjacent sides.
A parallelogram has opposite sides parallel and equal.
A regular polygon has all sides and angles equal.
A circle has a center and radius.
A tangent touches a circle at one point.
A tangent can touch a circle at two points.
Concentric circles have the same center.
A median of a triangle joins a vertex to the midpoint of opposite side.
An altitude of a triangle is perpendicular to opposite side.
A triangle can have three altitudes.
A triangle can have three medians.
The diagonal of a square = side × √2
The diagonal of a rectangle = √(length² + breadth²)
Constructing a square requires only a ruler.
Constructing a circle requires a compass.
Constructing a triangle requires three sides or two sides and included angle.
Constructing a rhombus requires two diagonals.
Constructing a kite requires adjacent sides equal.
Constructing a rectangle requires length and breadth.
Constructing a parallelogram requires two sides and included angle.
The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°
The sum of angles in a quadrilateral is 360°
Constructing a tangent from a point outside the circle involves perpendicular.
Constructing a circle through three points requires compass.
All triangles can be drawn using ruler and compass.
Some triangles cannot be drawn with given measurements.
A sector is part of a circle bounded by two radii and arc.
A segment is part of a circle bounded by a chord and arc.
A polygon can have curved sides.
All quadrilaterals have perpendicular diagonals.
The circumcenter is the intersection of perpendicular bisectors of triangle sides.
The incenter is the intersection of angle bisectors of triangle.
Practical geometry is useful in construction and engineering.
